Saint John the Hut-dweller
In the Lives of the Saints edited by Saint Nikodemos the Athonite, the
a short notice about John the Hut-dweller reads as follows:
. Commemoration of our holy father John the Hut-dweller.
. Denying the world, the boy left his earthly hut,
and in heavenly meadows, a new hut he pitched. ]
on This saint was born in Constantinople in the year 460 during the reign
oo of Emperor Leo the Great; his father was a senator named Eutropios and
| his mother was called Theodora. At an early age, indeed, while he was
still at elementary school, he secretly left his parents and teachers and, : joining himself to a monk, went to the monastery of the Unsleeping
. Order and assumed the monastic habit. There he exercised himself in an
oo exceedingly austere way of life. The devil, however, troubled the youth
Lo with love for his parents, and so the saint resolved to face and defeat the

- enemy. How and in what way? First the saint revealed everything about | i ‘himself to the abbat of the monastery and asked for his permission to return to his parents. And so, having received a blessing from each of the a monks in the community, he came to his fathers house as a pauper 4 oo dressed in rags and unrecognisable. As a beggar, he received from his fa- | . ther a tiny patch of land near the entrance to his home, and there he built

0 a tiny hut and dwelt there preserving his identity unknown. The blessed | . ‘man was pained when from his beggar’s shack he saw his parents pass- | ing before him with all their worldly vanity and, even worse, he was [ | obliged to endure insults and maltreatment from the servants,

After spending three years there with great self-denial and deprivaFE tion, he foresaw his coming death. Thereupon he called his mother and FE made himself known to her through a gilded Gospel book which his
parents had had made for him while he was still a schoolboy. As soon | ashehad made himself knows, the blessed man fell asleep and depart- | . ed to the Lord.
